title: ED syncope documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s
source.population: Adults and young people aged 16 years and older with suspected transient loss of consciousness.
source.setting: Initial assessment, emergency transfer, referral and specialist evaluation of transient loss of consciousness.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected event-history, witness-account, vital-sign, cardiovascular or neurological assessment, ECG-result, urgent-risk and transfer-of-record documentation. It is international guidance and does not establish the diagnosis or UAE disposition.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording what occurred before, during and after the event, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, injury, duration, confusion and focal weakness from patient and witness accounts.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-recorded urgent-risk context including ECG abnormalities, heart failure signs, exertional events, family history, unexplained breathlessness and murmur without generating urgency or referral.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.2.1–1.1.2.4 — history, examination, vital signs, ECG and selected tests

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports prior-event, medical, family and medicine history, measured vital signs, cardiovascular and neurological signs, ECG status and clinician-selected testing when an underlying problem is suspected.

assessment.text: Supports careful recording of all accounts and transfer of paramedic, ECG and patient-report records to the receiving clinician.
assessment.rationale: The exact record-transfer section supports recording existing ECG or report transfer, clinician documentation plan and arranged handover without selecting a destination or diagnosis.
